solutions.solution: Names of the squares are ☐ ABCX, ☐ DEFB', ☐ C'D'F'S', ☐ D'V'E'T', ☐ WXP'V, ☐ S'T'W'Q', ☐ T'E'U'W', ☐ G'GHH', ☐ TQ'RS, ☐ M'L'OP, ☐ L'K'NO, ☐ I'JKL, ☐ ACT'W, ☐ EGT', ☐ V'T'QS, ☐ T'GKO, ☐ AEKS, ☐ C'V'U'Q', ☐ WZN'U, ☐ ZT'R'N' ☐ T'P'QU', ☐ P'QIU'V, ☐ UN'QS, ☐ N'R'QO ☐ R'V'MO, ☐ J'IKM, ☐ ZP'MO. Hence, there are 27 squares in this figure. The above figure has following six parallelograms //ABDC, //ABFE, //BCED,//BCGF, //CDFE and //DEGF
Smallest squares are ☐ ABQP, ☐ BCRQ, ☐ CDSR, ☐ DEFS, ☐ PQTO, ☐ QRUT, ☐ RSVU, ☐ SFGV, ☐ OTWN, ☐ TUXW, ☐ VYXU, ☐ VGHY, ☐ NWLM, ☐ WXKL, ☐ YXJK, ☐ YHU = 16
Pentagons are ABCDH, CBAED, GAFED, BAFED, FDCBA, AFEDC. Hence, there are 6 pentagons.
Smallest hexagons = KBCMHI, LCDNGH = 2 Largest hexagons = KBDNGI = 1 ∴ Total hexagons = 2 + 1 = 3
